positive_description: Subtle upper-face adjustment — a slight widening of the eye region and mild brow smoothing. The forehead relaxes and the periorbital area opens marginally. The overall impression is a very subtle alertness or openness in the upper face, without dramatic brow movement.
negative_description: Subtle upper-face narrowing — the eye region contracts slightly and there is mild brow compression. The forehead tightens marginally. The overall impression is of a very subtle closing-down or guarding of the upper face.
symmetry_notes: Appears bilaterally symmetric based on the 3/4 views. Both sides show consistent deformation at +2.0. No clear left-right asymmetry detected.
artifact_notes: No mesh artifacts at any tested value. Very subtle component — would likely tolerate higher weights than primary components before artifacts appear. Conservative safe range set at +/-5.0.

interactions.psi2: Both affect upper face; psi2 drives dramatic mouth opening while psi13 provides subtle periorbital adjustment
interactions.psi7: psi7 drives eyelid-inclusive happy/disappointed axis; psi13 operates on the surrounding periorbital region more subtly

prose: Psi13 is a subtle upper-face modulator operating in the periorbital and forehead region. Its positive pole opens the eye area slightly — not a dramatic brow raise like psi2 but a gentle widening and smoothing of the forehead. Its negative pole does the opposite, producing a very mild narrowing and compression. The effects are among the most subtle of the mid-frequency components examined, requiring careful A/B comparison to detect. It represents the kind of fine-grained tonal adjustment that PCA captures in its mid-frequency band — micro-variations in upper face tension that contribute to expression authenticity but do not carry emotional valence on their own.
